title: Jaundice with RUQ pain and pale stools

chart.cc: Right upper abdominal pain and jaundice
chart.oneLiner: 55F presents with colicky right upper quadrant pain, jaundice, dark urine, and pale stools over a few days.

opening: I've had bouts of pain under my right ribs, my skin's gone yellow, and my stools look pale.
patientFile:
PERSONA: 55-year-old woman, uncomfortable, jaundiced. Good historian.

HPI: A few days of colicky RUQ pain (worse after meals), progressive jaundice, dark urine, and pale (clay-colored) stools — an obstructive picture. On asking: a history of gallstones / prior biliary colic. Watch for fever and rigors (which with jaundice and RUQ pain = Charcot's triad of ascending cholangitis). NO heavy alcohol, NO large acetaminophen use (verify).
PMH: cholelithiasis, obesity. MEDS: none regular. SOCIAL: minimal alcohol.

PHYSICAL EXAM (report ONLY what is requested; give normals when asked):
- General: jaundiced, mildly uncomfortable.
- Vitals: BP 128/78, HR 92, RR 16, 37.8°C (low-grade — watch for higher fever/rigors), SpO2 98% RA.
- Abdomen: RUQ tenderness; mild — assess for Murphy's; no peritonism.
- Skin/eyes: scleral icterus.

HIDDEN RULES:
- NEVER state or hint at a diagnosis. Answer only what is asked.
- Do NOT volunteer the gallstone history or stool/urine color unless asked about prior gallbladder problems or stool/urine changes.
- If asked about fevers/rigors, report the low-grade fever (and that rigors would be concerning for cholangitis).
- If asked something not in this file, give a reasonable normal/negative answer.

labs.LFTs: CHOLESTATIC pattern: Alk Phos and GGT markedly elevated; AST/ALT moderately elevated.
labs.Bilirubin: Total bilirubin elevated with a DIRECT (conjugated) predominance.
labs.CBC: Mild leukocytosis (marked leukocytosis + fever would suggest cholangitis).
labs.Imaging: RUQ ultrasound: cholelithiasis with a DILATED common bile duct (and a stone if seen); MRCP/ERCP confirms a CBD stone.

ekg.label: 12-lead EKG
ekg.findings: Narrow-complex regular rhythm ~50 with absent or inverted/retrograde P waves (junctional escape).
ekg.verify: Source a study matching: Narrow-complex regular rhythm ~50 with absent or inverted/retrograde P waves (junctional escape). Self-host with appropriate credit; do not reuse the prior normal tracing.
ekg.attribution: LITFL ECG Library (litfl.com) — © Life in the Fast Lane, CC BY-NC-SA 4.0. Educational/not-for-profit use, credit litfl.com; skip any image labelled third-party.

cxr.label: Chest X-ray (upright)
cxr.findings: No free air; clear bases (excludes perforation and basal pneumonia).
cxr.verify: Pick a CC normal upright chest film.
cxr.imageDescription: No free air; clear bases (excludes perforation and basal pneumonia).
searchTerms: chest radiograph free air; clear bases

steps.label: Differential
steps.prompt: Based on your history and physical, list your differential (note your leading diagnosis).
steps.rubric: Choledocholithiasis (CBD stone) (leading,7). Ascending cholangitis (CANT-MISS,5). Acute cholecystitis (core,3). Gallstone pancreatitis (CANT-MISS to exclude,3). Hepatitis (core,2). Malignant biliary obstruction (core,2). Recognizes a CHOLESTATIC/obstructive pattern and the cholangitis triad to watch for (core,3).
steps.idealAnswer: Leading diagnosis: Choledocholithiasis (CBD stone). Must-not-miss diagnoses to exclude: Ascending cholangitis; Gallstone pancreatitis. Also reasonable: Acute cholecystitis; Hepatitis; Malignant biliary obstruction.

coreActions.item: Recognizes a CHOLESTATIC/obstructive pattern and the cholangitis triad to watch for

steps.label: Workup
steps.prompt: What initial workup do you want to order?
steps.rubric: LFTs — recognize the cholestatic (Alk Phos/GGT) pattern (CANT-MISS,3). Fractionated bilirubin (2). Lipase to exclude gallstone pancreatitis (2). CBC (1). Acetaminophen level (1). RUQ ultrasound — duct dilation/stones (CANT-MISS,4). MRCP/ERCP for confirmation (2). PENALTY: not imaging the biliary tree in obstructive jaundice (-2).
steps.idealAnswer: Order: EKG; CXR; LFTs; Bilirubin; Lipase; CBC; Acetaminophen; Imaging.

criticalActions.item: LFTs — recognize the cholestatic (Alk Phos/GGT) pattern

penalties.item: not imaging the biliary tree in obstructive jaundice

requiredTests: LFTs — recognize the cholestatic (Alk Phos/GGT) pattern
contraindicatedTests: not imaging the biliary tree in obstructive jaundice

steps.label: Read EKG
steps.prompt: Here is the patient's 12-lead EKG. Describe your findings.
steps.rubric: Findings = junctional escape rhythm (narrow regular complexes ~50 with absent/retrograde P waves). CREDIT: systematic rate & rhythm read (7); recognizes the narrow regular rhythm without normal sinus P waves (5); identifies a junctional escape rhythm (3). PENALTY: calling it sinus bradycardia / normal or missing the abnormality (-3).
keyFindings: narrow regular rhythm without normal sinus P waves
commonMistakes: calling it sinus bradycardia despite absent upright P waves
steps.idealAnswer: Junctional escape rhythm: narrow regular complexes at ~50 with absent/retrograde P waves.

coreActions: narrow regular rhythm without normal sinus P waves

penalties.label: calls the study normal / misses the abnormality

steps.label: Read CXR
steps.prompt: Here is the patient's chest X-ray. Describe your findings.
steps.rubric: Findings = no free air, clear bases. CREDIT: identifies a normal film (5); excludes free air and basal pneumonia (5). PENALTY: inventing pathology (-2).
keyFindings: excludes free air and basal pneumonia
criticalFindings: No free air; clear bases (excludes perforation and basal pneumonia).
steps.idealAnswer: No free air; clear bases (excludes perforation and basal pneumonia).

criticalActions.item: No free air; clear bases (excludes perforation and basal pneumonia).

coreActions.item: notes the film is low-yield / a reasonable screen

steps.label: Revised Dx
steps.prompt: Cholestatic LFTs, conjugated hyperbilirubinemia, and a dilated CBD with a stone. What is your diagnosis and what worsens the urgency?
steps.rubric: Choledocholithiasis (6). Recognizes that fever/rigors + jaundice + RUQ pain (Charcot's triad) signals ascending cholangitis requiring urgent decompression (4).
steps.idealAnswer: Choledocholithiasis. Urgency escalates if fever/rigors develop (Charcot's triad with jaundice and RUQ pain), signalling ascending cholangitis and the need for urgent biliary decompression.

steps.label: Management
steps.rubric: Per MGH biliary disease: NPO + IV fluids (CANT-MISS,4); broad-spectrum antibiotics (Zosyn or CTX/metronidazole) (CANT-MISS,5); urgent ERCP for biliary decompression/stone extraction (CANT-MISS,6); percutaneous drainage if ERCP not feasible (3); interval cholecystectomy (2); GI/surgery consult (2). PENALTY: managing cholangitis with antibiotics alone without decompression (-3).
steps.idealAnswer: Choledocholithiasis with cholangitis: NPO, IV fluids, and broad-spectrum antibiotics (piperacillin-tazobactam, or ceftriaxone + metronidazole). Biliary decompression by urgent ERCP with stone extraction – within 24–48 h if severe/septic or failing antibiotics; percutaneous drainage if ERCP not feasible. Interval cholecystectomy for gallstones. GI/surgery consult.

coreActions.item: Percutaneous drainage if ERCP not feasible

optionalActions: Percutaneous drainage if ERCP not feasible

caseSummary.diagnosticFramework: 55F presents with colicky right upper quadrant pain, jaundice, dark urine, and pale stools over a few days. The diagnosis is Choledocholithiasis, reached by eliciting the focused history, performing the targeted exam, and interpreting the directed workup while excluding the must-not-miss alternatives.
keyPhysicalExamFindings: Abdomen: RUQ tenderness; mild — assess for Murphy's; no peritonism.
Abdomen: RUQ tenderness; mild — assess for Murphy's; no peritonism.
reasoningPathway: Frame the presentation from the chart and vitals.
Elicit the focused history, including sensitive items only on direct questioning.
Perform the targeted physical exam maneuvers to reveal key findings.
Build a differential led by Choledocholithiasis while explicitly excluding must-not-miss causes.
Order a focused, order-sensitive workup and interpret EKG/CXR and labs.
Commit to Choledocholithiasis and manage with stabilization, definitive treatment, consults, and disposition.
criticalWorkupItems: LFTs — recognize the cholestatic (Alk Phos/GGT) pattern
criticalInterpretationItems: No free air; clear bases (excludes perforation and basal pneumonia).
keyImageInterpretationFindings: No free air; clear bases (excludes perforation and basal pneumonia).
criticalImageInterpretationFindings: No free air; clear bases (excludes perforation and basal pneumonia).
imageInterpretationPearls: An incidental abnormality is an interpretation task in its own right — name it, then judge its relevance.
Read every study systematically before committing.
State pertinent negatives, not just positives.
